persona: A 29-year-old ceramic artist in Porto opening her second studio, coordinating a group exhibition with four other artists.

facts.text: met with her accountant Pedro to discuss tax implications for the new studio
spent 4 hours reviewing financial projections for the studio
had a meeting with fellow artist and friend Sofia to discuss their collaborative piece for the exhibition
felt excited about the prospect of showcasing her work in a new space
visited the Ribeira district to explore potential locations for a future workshop
sold 5 pieces of her work online, totaling 1200 euros

felt anxious about the upcoming exhibition and the pressure to deliver

transcripts.1:
Snap: Congratulations on the new studio, how's the setup going?
Star: It's been a whirlwind, to be honest. I met with my accountant Pedro yesterday to discuss the tax implications for the new studio, and I have to say, it was a real eye-opener. I had no idea how much of a difference it would make.

Snap: That sounds like a lot to take in. Did you have to do a lot of number-crunching to get your head around it?
Star: Yeah, I spent 4 hours reviewing financial projections for the studio, just to make sure I understood everything. It's not my favorite part of the job, but it's necessary, you know?

Snap: I can imagine. Who's been helping you with the more logistical side of things?
Star: Oh, my assistant Ana has been a lifesaver. We discussed studio logistics at length, from staffing to supply chains, and she's just been fantastic at helping me get everything organized.

Snap: That's great to have someone reliable on your team. What's the plan for the studio's first exhibition?
Star: Well, we're actually coordinating a group exhibition with four other artists, and I'm really excited about it. Ana's been helping me with the details, and Pedro's advice has been invaluable in making sure we're set up for success.

Snap: A group exhibition sounds like a fantastic way to launch the studio. How do you see this new space differing from your previous one?
Star: Honestly, I think the new studio will allow me to expand my creative vision, and with Ana's help, we can make it a really special place for artists and art lovers alike. And, of course, with Pedro's guidance, we'll be able to navigate the business side of things with confidence.

Snap: Last question: what are you most looking forward to about the new studio?
Star: I think it's just the opportunity to share my passion with the community, and to have a space where I can collaborate with other artists and learn from them. After all the planning and preparation, it's going to be amazing to see it all come together.
transcripts.2:
Snap: Congratulations on the upcoming exhibition and the new studio. How's the preparation going?
Star: It's going great, thanks for asking. I had a meeting with Sofia yesterday to discuss our collaborative piece, and I'm really looking forward to seeing it all come together. We've been friends for a while, and it's amazing to be working together on this project.
Snap: That sounds like a fantastic opportunity. What inspired you to collaborate with Sofia on this particular piece?
Star: We've always bounced ideas off each other, and this exhibition felt like the perfect chance to create something unique together. Plus, I think our styles complement each other really well.
Snap: I've heard great things about Sofia's work. What's the dynamic like when you two work together?
Star: It's always a lot of fun. We challenge each other to try new things, and I think that's what makes our collaboration so exciting. We're both really passionate about our art, and that energy is infectious.
Snap: Your passion is definitely evident. How does it feel to be showcasing your work in this new studio?
Star: Honestly, I'm really excited about the prospect of showcasing my work in a new space. It's a fresh start, and I feel like it's going to be a great opportunity for me to reach a new audience and connect with people who appreciate my art.
Snap: That's terrific. Are you nervous at all about the exhibition, or is it all excitement at this point?
Star: A little bit of both, I think. But mostly, I'm just eager to see everything come together and share my work with the community here in Porto.
Snap: Last question: what can our readers expect from your exhibition, and how does your collaboration with Sofia fit into the larger show?
Star: They can expect a really diverse and engaging show, with five different artists bringing their unique perspectives to the table. Sofia and I are contributing a piece that we're both really proud of, and I think it's going to be a great addition to the exhibition as a whole.
transcripts.3:
Snap: Congratulations on the upcoming group exhibition. What's been the highlight of your week so far?
Star: Honestly, it's been a whirlwind, but I did manage to sneak away to the Ribeira district yesterday to explore potential locations for a future workshop. The historic architecture and lively atmosphere are really inspiring, and I think it could be the perfect spot to set up something new.
Snap: That sounds amazing. The Ribeira district is so charming. Are you looking to expand your studio operations, or is this workshop going to be something separate from your current studio?
Star: Well, I'm not entirely sure yet, but I'm excited to have the opportunity to consider my options. On a more immediate note, I've been thrilled with the response to my online store – I sold 5 pieces of my work just yesterday, which is a huge boost, totaling 1200 euros. It's a great feeling knowing that my art is resonating with people.
Snap: Wow, that's fantastic news about your sales. How do you think the success of your online store will impact your plans for the new studio or workshop?
Star: It's definitely going to help inform my decisions. Knowing that there's a strong demand for my work online gives me the confidence to consider how I can expand my physical presence in Porto, whether that's through the new studio or potentially this future workshop in the Ribeira district.
Snap: I can imagine. The group exhibition is coming up soon. How are preparations going, and are you feeling any pressure with the online sales and the potential new workshop on the horizon?
Star: Preparations are going well, thanks for asking. It's a collaborative effort with four other talented artists, so it's been a pleasure working together. As for pressure, I think the recent online sales have actually helped alleviate some of the stress. It's a reminder that people appreciate my art, which motivates me to keep pushing forward with all my projects.
Snap: That's great to hear. Are you planning on showcasing any of the pieces that have been popular online at the group exhibition?
Star: Yes, I am. I think it will be a wonderful opportunity to connect with buyers and fans in person, especially after the success I've had online. It's going to be an exciting event, and I'm looking forward to seeing how people react to my work in a physical setting.
Snap: Last question: What can fans expect from this group exhibition that might be different from what they've seen of your work online?
Star: I think the exhibition will offer a more immersive experience. People will get to see the textures, the colors, and the details of my ceramics up close, which doesn't always translate through a screen. Plus, the variety of work from all five artists will make for a really engaging and dynamic show.
transcripts.4:
Snap: How's the new studio coming along, and what's the plan for the upcoming exhibition?
Star: It's been a whirlwind, to be honest. I've been putting in long hours to get everything just right. I actually reconnected with my mentor, artist Joana, the other day to seek her advice on studio management – she's been a huge help in keeping me organized and focused.
Snap: That's great, having a mentor like Joana must be invaluable. What's the most challenging part of this process for you right now?
Star: I'd say it's the pressure to deliver a great exhibition. I'm feeling a bit anxious about it, to be honest. There are a lot of expectations, and I want to make sure I do my best work.
Snap: Anxiety can be overwhelming. How do you plan to manage your nerves and ensure everything goes smoothly?
Star: Well, Joana reminded me that it's normal to feel this way, and that I've prepared well for this. She gave me some great tips on prioritizing tasks and staying calm under pressure, so I'm trying to focus on one thing at a time.
Snap: That's really helpful advice. Can you tell me more about the exhibition and what people can expect to see?
Star: It's a group exhibition with four other talented artists, and we're all bringing our unique perspectives to the table. I think it's going to be a fantastic show, and I'm excited to see how everything comes together.
Snap: A group exhibition can be a lot to coordinate. How are you finding the experience of working with the other artists?
Star: It's been great, actually. We all share a passion for our craft, and it's been wonderful to see our different styles and approaches come together. Of course, there are always some nerves when you're exhibiting your work, but I think we're all feeling the same pressure to deliver.
Snap: Last question: what do you hope people take away from the exhibition, and how do you think it will impact your career as a ceramic artist?
Star: I hope people enjoy the show and see the effort and dedication that's gone into creating each piece. As for me, I'm just taking it one step at a time and trying to learn as much as I can from the experience. The anxiety is still there, but I'm trying to channel it into something positive.
transcripts.5:
Snap: Congratulations on the upcoming group exhibition. How's the preparation going?
Star: It's going great, thanks. I actually met with the exhibition curator, Luis, yesterday to finalize the exhibition layout, and I'm really happy with how it's all coming together.

Snap: That sounds exciting. What about your own work? How's your latest piece coming along?
Star: I'm feeling really satisfied with the progress made on my latest ceramic piece. I've been putting in a lot of time and effort, and it's starting to pay off.

Snap: I can imagine. Ceramics can be a time-consuming process. How much time did you spend in the studio yesterday?
Star: Well, I spent 6 hours glazing and firing a new batch of ceramics, which was a big chunk of my day, but it was worth it to see everything come out of the kiln looking so great.

Snap: Wow, 6 hours is a long time. Did you take any breaks or was it intense focus the whole time?
Star: Yeah, I took a few short breaks, but mostly I was just in the zone, trying to get everything just right. It's always a bit nerve-wracking when you're firing a new batch, but Luis's feedback on the exhibition layout really helped me feel more confident about my work.

Snap: I bet. So, what can people expect to see from you at the exhibition? Will you have any pieces similar to the one you're working on now?
Star: Yeah, definitely. My latest piece is going to be one of the centerpiece pieces, and I think people are really going to love it. I'm just putting the final touches on it now, but I'm feeling really satisfied with how it's turning out.

Snap: Well, we can't wait to see it. How does it feel to be showing your work alongside four other artists? Is there a sense of camaraderie or competition?
Star: Oh, definitely camaraderie. We're all really supportive of each other, and I think that's going to come through in the exhibition. Luis has done a great job of curating the show to showcase each of our unique styles.

Snap: That sounds great. Finally, what's next for you after the exhibition opens? Will you be taking some time off or diving right into new projects?
Star: Well, I'll probably take a little time to catch my breath, but then I'll be back in the studio, working on some new pieces. I've got a few ideas kicking around in my head, and I'm excited to start experimenting with some new techniques.
transcripts.6:
Snap: Congratulations on the upcoming exhibition, how's the preparation going?
Star: It's been a whirlwind, but I'm feeling really proud of the progress we've made so far. Coordinating with the other artists to arrange transportation for the exhibition pieces has been a challenge, but we're all working together to make it happen.

Snap: That sounds like a lot of work. What's been the most difficult part of the process for you?
Star: Honestly, it's just making sure everything gets to the right place at the right time. But we're all in this together, and I'm excited to see it all come together. I have to say, having a great team of artists to work with makes all the difference.

Snap: I bet. So, have you had any visitors to the studio lately who've given you any valuable feedback or advice?
Star: Actually, my friend Maria came by the other day. She's also an artist, and we're going to be sharing the studio space, so it was great to catch up with her and discuss the details of how we'll be using the space. She had some really great insights on the layout and how we can make the most of it.

Snap: Maria, that's a great connection to have. What's the dynamic like between you and the other artists in the exhibition?
Star: It's really collaborative. We've all been working together to make sure the exhibition is a success, and I think that's what's going to make it so special. We're all bringing our own unique perspectives and styles to the table, and I think the audience is really going to respond to that.

Snap: That sounds fantastic. What do you think is going to be the standout piece of the exhibition?
Star: I think it's going to be a tough call, because everyone's work is so strong. But I'm really proud of the pieces I've been working on, and I think they're going to resonate with the audience. Plus, seeing everything come together with the other artists' work is going to be amazing.

Snap: Well, we're all looking forward to seeing it. How's the studio itself coming along, is it going to be ready on time?
Star: Yeah, I'm feeling really proud of the progress we've made on the studio. It's going to be a great space, and I think it's going to be the perfect venue for the exhibition. We're all working hard to make sure everything is ready to go for the opening.

Snap: Last question, what are you most looking forward to about the exhibition?
Star: I think it's just going to be amazing to see all of our hard work pay off. I'm really proud of what we've accomplished, and I think the audience is going to love it. Plus, getting to share it with Maria and the other artists is going to make it a really special experience.
transcripts.7:
Snap: Congratulations on the upcoming exhibition and your second studio. How's the preparation going so far?
Star: You know, it's been a whirlwind, but I felt relieved that we're nearing completion. I spent 3 hours responding to emails and messages from potential clients and exhibition attendees, just making sure everything is in place.

Snap: That sounds like a lot of work. Have you had any help or support from your team or partners?
Star: Yeah, I had a phone call with my business partner yesterday to discuss marketing strategies for the studio. We're really trying to get the word out and bring in some new faces.

Snap: Marketing is key. What inspires your work and have you found any new ideas lately?
Star: Actually, I visited the Serralves Museum yesterday and saw the current ceramics exhibition. It was amazing, really got my creative juices flowing. I love seeing what other artists are doing and it always seems to spark something new in me.

Snap: The Serralves Museum is a great place to find inspiration. How's your online presence looking? Are you getting a lot of interest on social media?
Star: Yeah, it's been great. I tracked 25 new followers on my social media account just yesterday, so that's really helping to increase my online presence. It's nice to see that people are interested in my work and the exhibition.

Snap: That's fantastic. Do you think your online presence will translate to foot traffic in the studio?
Star: I hope so. We'll have to wait and see, but I'm feeling positive. The exhibition is going to be a great opportunity for people to see my work and the work of the other four artists involved, so I'm keeping my fingers crossed.

Snap: Well, we're all looking forward to it. What's the most important thing for you in these final days before the exhibition opens?
Star: Just making sure everything is perfect, you know? But like I said, I'm feeling relieved that we're almost there, so I think we're on the right track.

Snap: Last question: what can people expect from the exhibition and your new studio?
Star: They can expect to see some amazing ceramics from five very talented artists. We're all really excited to showcase our work and I think people are going to love it.
